question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a crafted protobuf Transaction with an artificially large number of sigPair entries in the SignatureMap to inflate numTxnSignatures in TransactionFeeContext, causing the fee estimate to exceed the true on-chain fee and mislead callers into overpaying?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user set highVolumeThrottle=10000 (the @Max boundary) combined with a transaction body that sets highVolume=true to force the fee calculator to apply the maximum high-volume multiplier, producing a fee estimate that is orders of magnitude above the baseline and outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user set highVolumeThrottle=0 while also setting highVolume=true in the transaction body to suppress the high-volume multiplier entirely, causing the fee estimate to be artificially lower than the actual on-chain fee and enabling underpriced transaction planning?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a Transaction with an extremely large body (e.g., a memo field padded to the maximum allowed size) to inflate numTxnBytes() via Transaction.PROTOBUF.measureRecord(), causing the byte-based fee component to be computed at an inflated value outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a Transaction using the legacy bodyBytes path (instead of signedTransactionBytes) with a crafted SignatureMap containing many sigPairs to inflate numTxnSignatures in TransactionFeeContext, bypassing any signature-count assumptions baked into the fee schedule?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user switch mode=STATE vs mode=INTRINSIC to obtain two different fee estimates for the same transaction, and by choosing the lower estimate, plan a transaction that will be rejected on-chain due to insufficient fees, effectively causing a denial-of-service on their own transaction flow?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a Transaction with mode=INTRINSIC where getHighVolumeThrottleUtilization() always returns 0 regardless of the highVolumeThrottle query parameter, causing the high-volume multiplier to be silently ignored and producing a fee estimate that does not reflect actual network congestion?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: toResponse()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger an integer overflow in toExtra() where subtotal is computed as detail.perUnit() * detail.charged() with no overflow guard, causing the reported fee subtotal to wrap to a negative or near-zero value and mislead callers about the true fee?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: toResponse()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction type that produces a FeeResult with a very large getHighVolumeMultiplier() value, and when divided by HIGH_VOLUME_MULTIPLIER_SCALE in toResponse(), result in a truncated integer that misrepresents the actual multiplier applied to the fee?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror-node--039/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a Transaction whose signedTransactionBytes contains a valid outer wrapper but whose inner bodyBytes is a zero-length or minimal protobuf, causing the fee calculator to compute a near-zero fee estimate that does not reflect the actual minimum fee enforced on-chain?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a Transaction with both signedTransactionBytes and bodyBytes populated, and by exploiting the priority logic in TransactionFeeContext (signedTransactionBytes is checked first), cause the fee to be calculated against a different transaction body than the one in bodyBytes, producing a mismatched fee estimate?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getFees()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a future timestamp (e.g., timestamp=gte:99999999999) to getEffectiveFeeSchedule() in FeeScheduleMapper, forcing the response to return the nextFeeSchedule instead of the currentFeeSchedule, thereby leaking future fee parameters before they are officially active?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getFees()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a future timestamp to getEffectiveExchangeRate() in FeeScheduleMapper, forcing the response to use the nextRate exchange rate instead of the currentRate, causing the reported gas-to-tinybars conversion to reflect a future rate and mislead fee planning?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getFees()] Can an unprivileged external user supply two timestamp parameters (e.g., timestamp=gt:0&timestamp=lt:1) that produce a Bound whose adjustUpperBound() returns a value just before the current fee schedule's expiry, causing getEffectiveFeeSchedule() to return the currentFeeSchedule with a lower gas price than the nextFeeSchedule, producing a fee underestimate?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getFees()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a timestamp just after the currentRate expiration in getEffectiveExchangeRate() to force the nextRate to be used, and if the nextRate has a higher hbarEquiv/centEquiv ratio, cause the reported gas fee in tinybars to be inflated beyond the current design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getFees()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a timestamp just before the currentRate expiration to force the currentRate to be used, and if the currentRate has a lower hbarEquiv/centEquiv ratio, cause the reported gas fee in tinybars to be deflated, misleading callers into submitting transactions with insufficient fees?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getFees()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the FEE_DIVISOR_FACTOR=1000L integer truncation in convertGasPriceToTinyBars() by querying a fee schedule where gasPrice < 1000, causing gasInTinyCents to truncate to 0 and the returned fee to always be Math.max(0, 1L)=1 tinycent, producing a fee estimate far below the actual on-chain cost?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getFees()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Math.max(gasInTinyBars, 1L) floor in convertGasPriceToTinyBars() to always receive a minimum fee of 1 tinycent regardless of the actual gas price, causing the fee estimate to be artificially floored and outside design parameters when the true fee should be 0?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getFees()] Can an unprivileged external user supply timestamp=lt:1 to getFees() to force Bound.adjustUpperBound() to return 0 (1-1=0), causing getEffectiveFeeSchedule() to compare 0 against the fee schedule expiry and potentially return the nextFeeSchedule when the currentFeeSchedule should apply, producing incorrect fee data?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a Transaction with a SignedTransaction whose sigMap contains duplicate or malformed sigPair entries to inflate numTxnSignatures beyond the actual number of valid signatures, causing the fee estimate to be higher than the true on-chain fee?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a Transaction with highVolumeThrottle=9999 (one below the @Max boundary) to probe the boundary behavior of the HighVolumePricingCalculator and determine whether the fee multiplier changes non-linearly near the maximum, enabling fee manipulation by choosing specific throttle values?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a Transaction with highVolumeThrottle=1 (just above the @Min boundary) to determine the minimum non-zero high-volume multiplier applied by HighVolumePricingCalculator, enabling precise fee manipulation by selecting the lowest possible throttle value that still activates high-volume pricing?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a Transaction body that specifies an unknown or unsupported HederaFunctionality type, causing FeeEstimationService.estimateFees() to throw IllegalArgumentException('Unknown transaction type') and leak information about which transaction types are supported or unsupported by the fee calculator?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a maximally large protobuf body (up to the server's request size limit) to cause numTxnBytes() via Transaction.PROTOBUF.measureRecord() to return an extremely large value, driving the byte-based fee component to its maximum and producing a fee estimate that exceeds the on-chain maximum fee?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a Transaction with mode=STATE and highVolumeThrottle=10000 but without setting highVolume=true in the transaction body, causing the high-volume throttle parameter to be silently ignored and the fee estimate to not reflect the congestion level the caller intended to simulate?
